verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est pauper qui ambulat in simplicitate sua  quam dives torquens labia sua, et insipiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טֽוֹב־רָ֭שׁ הוֹלֵ֣ךְ בְּתֻמּ֑וֹ מֵעִקֵּ֥שׁ שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו וְה֣וּא כְסִֽיל׃
verses.king_james: Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse in his lips, and is a fool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi non est scientia animæ, non est bonum,  et qui festinus est pedibus offendet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֤ם בְּלֹא־דַ֣עַת נֶ֣פֶשׁ לֹא־ט֑וֹב וְאָ֖ץ בְּרַגְלַ֣יִם חוֹטֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: Also, that the soul be without knowledge, it is not good; and he that hasteth with his feet sinneth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultitia hominis supplantat gressus ejus,  et contra Deum fervet animo suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִוֶּ֣לֶת אָ֭דָם תְּסַלֵּ֣ף דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וְעַל־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה יִזְעַ֥ף לִבּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divitiæ addunt amicos plurimos ;  a paupere autem et hi quos habuit separantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֗וֹן יֹ֭סִיף רֵעִ֣ים רַבִּ֑ים וְ֝דָ֗ל מֵרֵעֵ֥הוּ יִפָּרֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: Wealth maketh many friends; but the poor is separated from his neighbour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testis falsus non erit impunitus,  et qui mendacia loquitur non effugiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֣ד שְׁ֭קָרִים לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶ֑ה וְיָפִ֥יחַ כְּ֝זָבִ֗ים לֹ֣א יִמָּלֵֽט׃
verses.king_james: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall not escape.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi colunt personam potentis,  et amici sunt dona tribuentis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֭בִּים יְחַלּ֣וּ פְנֵֽי־נָדִ֑יב וְכׇל־הָ֝רֵ֗עַ לְאִ֣ישׁ מַתָּֽן׃
verses.king_james: Many will intreat the favour of the prince: and every man is a friend to him that giveth gifts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fratres hominis pauperis oderunt eum ;  insuper et amici procul recesserunt ab eo.      Qui tantum verba sectatur nihil habebit ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּ֥ל־אֲחֵי־רָ֨שׁ ׀ שְֽׂנֵאֻ֗הוּ אַ֤ף כִּ֣י מְ֭רֵעֵהוּ רָחֲק֣וּ מִמֶּ֑נּוּ מְרַדֵּ֖ף אֲמָרִ֣ים (לא) [לוֹ־]הֵֽמָּה׃
verses.king_james: All the brethren of the poor do hate him: how much more do his friends go far from him? he pursueth them with words, yet they are wanting to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui autem possessor est mentis diligit animam suam,  et custos prudentiæ inveniet bona.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֹֽנֶה־לֵּ֭ב אֹהֵ֣ב נַפְשׁ֑וֹ שֹׁמֵ֥ר תְּ֝בוּנָ֗ה לִמְצֹא־טֽוֹב׃
verses.king_james: He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Falsus testis non erit impunitus,  et qui loquitur mendacia peribit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֣ד שְׁ֭קָרִים לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶ֑ה וְיָפִ֖יחַ כְּזָבִ֣ים יֹאבֵֽד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non decent stultum deliciæ,  nec servum dominari principibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־נָאוֶ֣ה לִכְסִ֣יל תַּעֲנ֑וּג אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־לְעֶ֤בֶד ׀ מְשֹׁ֬ל בְּשָׂרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Delight is not seemly for a fool; much less for a servant to have rule over princes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Doctrina viri per patientiam noscitur,  et gloria ejus est iniqua prætergredi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֵׂ֣כֶל אָ֭דָם הֶאֱרִ֣יךְ אַפּ֑וֹ וְ֝תִפְאַרְתּ֗וֹ עֲבֹ֣ר עַל־פָּֽשַׁע׃
verses.king_james: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut fremitus leonis, ita et regis ira,  et sicut ros super herbam, ita et hilaritas ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֣הַם כַּ֭כְּפִיר זַ֣עַף מֶ֑לֶךְ וּכְטַ֖ל עַל־עֵ֣שֶׂב רְצוֹנֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The king’s wrath is as the roaring of a lion; but his favour is as dew upon the grass.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dolor patris filius stultus,  et tecta jugiter perstillantia litigiosa mulier.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַוֺּ֣ת לְ֭אָבִיו בֵּ֣ן כְּסִ֑יל וְדֶ֥לֶף טֹ֝רֵ֗ד מִדְיְנֵ֥י אִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.king_james: A foolish son is the calamity of his father: and the contentions of a wife are a continual dropping.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domus et divitiæ dantur a parentibus ;  a Domino autem proprie uxor prudens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֣יִת וָ֭הוֹן נַחֲלַ֣ת אָב֑וֹת וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה אִשָּׁ֥ה מַשְׂכָּֽלֶת׃
verses.king_james: House and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pigredo immittit soporem,  et anima dissoluta esuriet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֭צְלָה תַּפִּ֣יל תַּרְדֵּמָ֑ה וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ רְמִיָּ֣ה תִרְעָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Slothfulness casteth into a deep sleep; and an idle soul shall suffer hunger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui custodit mandatum custodit animam suam ;  qui autem negligit viam suam mortificabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֹׁמֵ֣ר מִ֭צְוָה שֹׁמֵ֣ר נַפְשׁ֑וֹ בּוֹזֵ֖ה דְרָכָ֣יו (יומת) [יָמֽוּת]׃
verses.king_james: He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fœneratur Domino qui miseretur pauperis,  et vicissitudinem suam reddet ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַלְוֵ֣ה יְ֭הֹוָה ח֣וֹנֵֽן דָּ֑ל וּ֝גְמֻל֗וֹ יְשַׁלֶּם־לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the Lord; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erudi filium tuum ; ne desperes :  ad interfectionem autem ejus ne ponas animam tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַסֵּ֣ר בִּ֭נְךָ כִּֽי־יֵ֣שׁ תִּקְוָ֑ה וְאֶל־הֲ֝מִית֗וֹ אַל־תִּשָּׂ֥א נַפְשֶֽׁךָ׃
verses.king_james: Chasten thy son while there is hope, and let not thy soul spare for his crying.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui impatiens est sustinebit damnum,  et cum rapuerit, aliud apponet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (גרל) [גְּֽדׇל־]חֵ֭מָה נֹ֣שֵׂא עֹ֑נֶשׁ כִּ֥י אִם־תַּ֝צִּ֗יל וְע֣וֹד תּוֹסִֽף׃
verses.king_james: A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment: for if thou deliver him, yet thou must do it again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audi consilium, et suscipe disciplinam,  ut sis sapiens in novissimis tuis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמַ֣ע עֵ֭צָה וְקַבֵּ֣ל מוּסָ֑ר לְ֝מַ֗עַן תֶּחְכַּ֥ם בְּאַחֲרִיתֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multæ cogitationes in corde viri ;  voluntas autem Domini permanebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַבּ֣וֹת מַחֲשָׁב֣וֹת בְּלֶב־אִ֑ישׁ וַעֲצַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה הִ֣יא תָקֽוּם׃
verses.king_james: There are many devices in a man’s heart; nevertheless the counsel of the Lord, that shall stand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo indigens misericors est,  et melior est pauper quam vir mendax.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּאֲוַ֣ת אָדָ֣ם חַסְדּ֑וֹ וְטֽוֹב־רָ֝֗שׁ מֵאִ֥ישׁ כָּזָֽב׃
verses.king_james: The desire of a man is his kindness: and a poor man is better than a liar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Timor Domini ad vitam,  et in plenitudine commorabitur absque visitatione pessima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֣ה לְחַיִּ֑ים וְשָׂבֵ֥עַ יָ֝לִ֗ין בַּל־יִפָּ֥קֶד רָֽע׃
verses.king_james: The fear of the Lord tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abscondit piger manum suam sub ascella,  nec ad os suum applicat eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טָ֘מַ֤ן עָצֵ֣ל יָ֭דוֹ בַּצַּלָּ֑חַת גַּם־אֶל־פִּ֝֗יהוּ לֹ֣א יְשִׁיבֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: A slothful man hideth his hand in his bosom, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pestilente flagellato stultus sapientior erit ;  si autem corripueris sapientem, intelliget disciplinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ץ תַּ֭כֶּה וּפֶ֣תִי יַעְרִ֑ם וְהוֹכִ֥יחַ לְ֝נָב֗וֹן יָבִ֥ין דָּֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware: and reprove one that hath understanding, and he will understand knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui affligit patrem, et fugat matrem,  ignominiosus est et infelix.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְֽשַׁדֶּד־אָ֭ב יַבְרִ֣יחַ אֵ֑ם בֵּ֝֗ן מֵבִ֥ישׁ וּמַחְפִּֽיר׃
verses.king_james: He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non cesses, fili, audire doctrinam,  nec ignores sermones scientiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲֽדַל־בְּ֭נִי לִשְׁמֹ֣עַ מוּסָ֑ר לִ֝שְׁג֗וֹת מֵאִמְרֵי־דָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: Cease, my son, to hear the instruction that causeth to err from the words of knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testis iniquus deridet judicium,  et os impiorum devorat iniquitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֣ד בְּ֭לִיַּעַל יָלִ֣יץ מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים יְבַלַּע־אָֽוֶן׃
verses.king_james: An ungodly witness scorneth judgment: and the mouth of the wicked devoureth iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Parata sunt derisoribus judicia,  et mallei percutientes stultorum corporibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָכ֣וֹנוּ לַלֵּצִ֣ים שְׁפָטִ֑ים וּ֝מַהֲלֻמ֗וֹת לְגֵ֣ו כְּסִילִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Judgments are prepared for scorners, and stripes for the back of fools.
